product_definition.product_description: Airbnb is a global online marketplace connecting travelers with unique short-term accommodation hosted by local hosts — from spare rooms to entire villas, treehouses, and castles.
product_definition.core_problem_solved: Generic hotel stays feel impersonal and expensive; travelers want authentic local experiences while hosts want to monetize their unused space.
product_definition.unique_selling_proposition: The only platform that turns any space into a story — enabling travelers to live like locals anywhere in the world, while giving hosts a trusted income stream.

target_audience.demographics: Millennials and Gen Z travelers (ages 24-40), urban professionals, couples, and remote workers. Secondary: property owners aged 30-55 looking to earn supplemental income.
target_audience.psychographics: Experience-seekers who value authenticity over luxury, community-minded, digitally native, skeptical of corporate travel brands, value transparency and reviews.
target_audience.cultural_nuances: Post-pandemic audience craving human connection and 'slow travel'; growing distrust of opaque pricing and hidden fees — demand full cost transparency upfront.

market_context.current_marketing_efforts: Brand campaigns ('Live Anywhere'), influencer partnerships, social media UGC from hosts and guests, email retargeting, and app-store optimization.
market_context.known_customer_objections: Fear of scams or misrepresented listings; cleaning fee sticker shock; preference for hotel consistency and loyalty points; safety concerns when staying with strangers.

the_creative_goal.primary_objective: Rebuild trust and re-ignite booking intent among lapsed users deterred by hidden fees and inconsistent quality — driving a 20% increase in first-time bookings.
the_creative_goal.desired_tone_of_voice: Warm, Human, Trustworthy, Adventurous, yet Grounded.
